incorrects.description: Student selected 2: may have miscounted or confused groups with items in one bag

params.description: All 3 bags highlight to draw attention to each group.

steps.text: Count the bags. Each bag is one group. How many bags do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4: counted items in one bag instead of counting groups

steps.text: You counted the items in one bag. The question asks how many groups. Count the bags, not what's inside.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6: may have miscounted or added bags and items incorrectly

steps.text: Look closely at the bags. Each bag is one group. Count them one at a time.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts each bag one by one, highlighting them in sequence: 1, 2, 3.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags: one bag, two bags, three bags. Each bag is a group. There are 3 groups. When we ask how many groups, we count the containers, not the items inside.
